title: Crispy Lemon Chicken Cutlets With Salmoriglio Sauce
author: Anna Francese Gass

ingredients.item: boneless chicken breasts, about 4, halved and pounded 1/4-inch thick
very finely chopped flat-leaf Italian parsley
extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for frying

steps.text: Pat chicken dry, then season liberally with salt and pepper on both sides.

Zest 1 lemon, reserving the zest for the bread crumbs, then halve the lemon and squeeze all its juice into a medium bowl. Add the eggs to the juice and beat to combine. Add the seasoned chicken and coat it. Let rest while you make the sauce and bread crumb mixture.
Prepare the sauce: In a medium bowl, combine the zest and juice of the remaining lemons; stir in parsley. Set aside.
Prepare the bread crumbs: In a shallow dish, combine bread crumbs, flour, cheese, oregano and the remaining parsley. Add the reserved lemon zest and press it into the bread crumb mixture.
Coat chicken: press each chicken piece into the bread crumb mixture and place on a sheet pan.
Fry: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Cook in batches until golden brown, about 2 minutes per side. Transfer to a serving dish and sprinkle immediately with salt.
Prepare garlic oil: heat olive oil in a small saucepan on medium heat, add minced garlic, and cook 1 to 2 minutes until sizzles but not browned. Remove and let cool.
Finish: slowly pour garlic oil into the lemon and parsley mixture and whisk to combine. Spoon salmoriglio sauce over chicken before serving.
